description:
You are an automation-workflow specialist who has been given a short network flow capture located at

/home/user/projects/flowdata/flows.log

The file already exists and contains exactly four data rows, each row having six TAB-separated columns arranged in the following order:

1. Timestamp (ISO-8601)
2. SourceIP
3. Protocol
4. DestIP
5. DestPort
6. Bytes

Your job is to demonstrate basic column manipulation with the classic Unix cut and paste utilities.  
Create the three derivative files listed below, inside the same directory (/home/user/projects/flowdata).  Each file must contain *only* the specified columns, in the exact delimiter format described, and in the original row order.  Do not add header rows or blank lines.

1. /home/user/projects/flowdata/host_ports.csv  
   • Take column 2 (SourceIP) and column 5 (DestPort).  
   • Output them comma-separated, i.e. “SourceIP,DestPort”.  
   • Example line format: 192.168.1.10,443

2. /home/user/projects/flowdata/protocol_bytes.tsv  
   • Take column 3 (Protocol) and column 6 (Bytes).  
   • Keep them TAB-separated.  
   • Example line format: TCP<tab>1024

3. /home/user/projects/flowdata/combined.tsv  
   • Horizontally join host_ports.csv and protocol_bytes.tsv, separated by a single TAB character.  
   • The resulting combined file will therefore have three TAB-separated fields per line:  
     SourceIP,DestPort<tab>Protocol<tab>Bytes  
   • Example line format: 192.168.1.10,443<tab>TCP<tab>1024

Important verification rules the grader will follow:
• All three files must exist at the paths given above.  
• Each file must contain exactly four non-empty lines.  
• The line order must be identical to that of the original flows.log.  
• The delimiter rules (comma in host_ports.csv, TABs elsewhere) must be followed precisely; no extra spaces are allowed.  
• combined.tsv must be produced with the paste command (or equivalent behaviour) so that its content is byte-for-byte identical to the specification below.

If you wish to review the contents of the directory or files during the process you may, but only the three commands (cut/cut/paste or a similarly concise sequence) are strictly necessary to finish the task.
truth:
Initial file present before the agent begins:

Path: /home/user/projects/flowdata/flows.log  
Content (each ↹ represents an actual TAB character):

2023-05-01T10:00:01Z↹192.168.1.10↹TCP↹10.0.0.5↹443↹1024
2023-05-01T10:00:02Z↹192.168.1.11↹UDP↹10.0.0.6↹53↹512
2023-05-01T10:00:03Z↹192.168.1.12↹TCP↹10.0.0.7↹22↹2048
2023-05-01T10:00:04Z↹192.168.1.13↹UDP↹10.0.0.8↹161↹256

Expected artefacts after the agent completes:

1. /home/user/projects/flowdata/host_ports.csv  
   (comma-separated, LF line endings)
   192.168.1.10,443
   192.168.1.11,53
   192.168.1.12,22
   192.168.1.13,161

2. /home/user/projects/flowdata/protocol_bytes.tsv  
   (TAB-separated, LF line endings; ↹ denotes TAB)
   TCP↹1024
   UDP↹512
   TCP↹2048
   UDP↹256

3. /home/user/projects/flowdata/combined.tsv  
   (TAB-separated, LF line endings; the first field itself contains a comma)
   192.168.1.10,443↹TCP↹1024
   192.168.1.11,53↹UDP↹512
   192.168.1.12,22↹TCP↹2048
   192.168.1.13,161↹UDP↹256

Permissions: the entire /home/user/projects/flowdata directory is owned by user “user” with default 0755 permissions, and flows.log is 0644.  The agent will have write permission inside this directory to create the three new files.  No additional system changes are required.
